1.11a: Address: Aldermore, 4th Floor, 40 Spring Gardens, Manchester, M2 1EN
Email for Individuals (Residential and BTL): Mortgage.Completions@aldermore.co.uk
Email for Companies, HMOs, and MUFs: BTLcompletions@aldermore.co.uk
Certificate of Title subject field requirement: Must contain 'COT' and the reference number
1.11b: Contact details are identical to section 1.11a
1.7: Lending is not permitted where the borrower and mortgagor are not the same entity
1.14: The firm may not act if the transaction handler or their immediate family is the seller
1.15: The firm may not act if the transaction handler or their immediate family is the borrower
3.1.3: Lender does not require notification of seller's solicitor details
Conveyancer must verify seller's solicitor is registered and has valid practising certificates
3.1.4: Notify lender if the seller is unrepresented or if checks on the seller's representative are unsatisfactory
Contact details are the same as section 1.11a
3.1.5: No other identity verification documents are accepted
3.2.3: Lender does not require notification of seller's solicitor details
Conveyancer must verify seller's solicitor is registered and has valid practising certificates
3.2.4: Notify lender if the seller is unrepresented or if checks on the seller's representative are unsatisfactory
Contact details are the same as section 1.11a
4.5a: Contact details for re-inspection are the same as section 1.11a
4.5b: Certificate of Title must be submitted with at least 3 working days' notice via email
Subject line for COT must include the reference number and 'COT'
Undervalue purchases require a completed Gifted Equity and Solvency Declaration Form
Insolvency Act Indemnity Insurance is mandatory for equity gifts
Customers must confirm understanding of tax obligations and provide copies of any independent tax advice
Separate legal representation is required for purchases from family members; the firm cannot act for both parties
4.1: Lender does not arrange inspections in every case
Conveyancer should use the valuation report for checks if provided, otherwise use the mortgage offer
Do not request a valuation report if one was not provided
4.3: Contact point for incorrect valuation assumptions is the same as section 1.11a
5.1.1: Property ownership of less than 6 months is generally not acceptable
Exceptions include purchases from housebuilders/developers via part-exchange
Remortgages within 6 months are acceptable if property was inherited, replaces bridging finance (>1 month old), or was an arm's length cash purchase (>1 month old) of a single unit
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and assignable contracts are not acceptable
Lease grants immediately following freehold acquisition are considered sub-sales
Maximum loan may be based on market value
5.2.1: Contact point for issues where the seller is not the registered proprietor is the same as 1.11a
5.4.4: Environmental reports are only required if they indicate risks to the security or require further investigation
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm is registered under The Search Code monitored by the PCCB
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted only for remortgages or personal-to-company transfers with party continuity
Search insurance is limited to local authority searches
Insurance must cover successors in title
5.5.3a: Title splits must be registered before completion
Contact point for reporting breaches is the same as section 1.11a
5.5.3b: Borrowers are to retain original or copies of planning permission
5.5.3c: Borrowers are to retain original or copies of building regulation consents
5.5.3d: Borrowers are to retain certificates of lawful use or development
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable; if not in valuation, refer to lender for check
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted except for coach house flats (one flat over garages/access)
5.8.1: Buildings converted into up to four flats are accepted if the borrower occupies one and owns the freehold
Report arrangements to lender if not in valuation report
5.8.5: Mutual leasehold/freehold reversion arrangements (two flats) are accepted if security includes both the leasehold and the freehold reversion of the other flat
5.13.1: Gifted equity from close relatives is capped at 50% of market value
Gifted equity from landlord to sitting tenant is capped at 25% of market value
Undervalue transfers require a Solvency Declaration and Insolvency Act Indemnity Insurance
Clear bankruptcy searches required for the borrower and all deposit contributors
5.14.1: Second-hand property minimum lease: 85 years at start, 45 years remaining at term end
New build houses minimum lease: 250 years
New build flats minimum lease: 125 years
5.14.9: Maximum starting ground rent: greater of £500 or 0.1% of property value
Ground rent must be indexed (e.g., RPI) or double no more frequently than every 20 years
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted if section 9 requirements are met
5.14.12: Borrowers are to retain ground rent and service charge receipts
5.14.13: Copy of receipted notice or evidence of service must be provided to lender after completion
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent or insolvent landlords is accepted
5.14.17b: Lender must be notified if the owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder under Building Safety Act 2022
Lender must be notified of any anticipated remediation costs or incorrect works identified
Completion must not occur until written instructions are received following a Building Safety Act report
5.15.2a: Estate rent charges are acceptable if: Section 121 LPA is excluded; OR a 28-day mortgagee protection clause is included; OR the owner is a resident-owned management company
Indemnity insurance only accepted for historic rent charges not collected for 12+ years
5.15.2c: Supply a copy of the signed blank stock transfer form to the lender after completion
5.17.5a: Power of Attorney is not normally accepted
5.19.1: The only acceptable affordable housing scheme is Help to Buy: equity loan
5.20.3: Notify lender of solar PV roof space leases and provide the tenant name
Copies of the lease are not required
5.20.4: Details of existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed
6.3.1: Purchase price may only be lower than market value for close relatives (max 50% discount) or sitting tenants (max 25% discount)
Transfer deed must reflect the actual price paid at completion
6.4.4: Incentives must be disclosed via the UK Finance Disclosure of Incentives Form
Finders fees or commissions to third parties (investment clubs) are not acceptable
Seller-paid deposits on non-new build properties must be reported
6.6.3: AST or SAT agreements up to 3 years are accepted
Scottish Private Residential Tenancies and Welsh Standard Occupation Contracts are accepted
Corporate lets to PLCs or social housing providers are accepted for up to 5 years
6.7.1: Acceptable warranties: ABC+, Advantage HCI, Aedis, Ark, Build Assure, Build Life Plans, Build Zone, Castle 10/Checkmate, CRL (if underwritten by ARK/IGI/CGICE), Global Home, ICW, LABC, NHBC, One Guarantee, Premier Guarantee, Protek, Q Assure, CADIS, Build warranty group
6.7.4: Properties without warranty accepted if <= 15 units in development and monitored by a professional consultant
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate must be sent to lender after completion
6.8.1: Bond/agreement for unadopted roads/sewers not required for new properties on completion if customer accepts
Refer to lender if there are no plans to ever obtain a bond/agreement
6.13.1: Improvement grants require a Local Authority waiver of repayment priority
Local Authority must undertake to remove land charge entries once repaid or sold by lender
7.3: Occupants aged 17 or over must sign a standard consent form unless it is a BTL loan
Homes for Ukraine occupants do not require consent or postponement forms
8.1: Advice to third parties must be provided by a different solicitor within the practice
Non-benefiting borrowers must execute a Certificate of Independent Advice
9.2: Indemnity insurance limit: 110% of purchase price or valuation (whichever is higher)
10.2b: Minimum 3 working days' notice required for the advance
Certificate of Title must be emailed to the completions team
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned electronically within 3 working days

Account for 'X' reference: Sort Code 20-19-90, Account 00103578

14.2.2: Post-completion documents: TID, Amended Articles (if applicable), Occupier's Consent, Notice to Landlord, Certificate of Independent Advice, Architect's Certificate, Indemnity policies, Fixed & Floating Charge + registration certificate, Guarantees, Board Minutes, Share Certificate/Stock Transfer Form, Deed of Subordination, Assignment of Rental Income, Gifted Equity and Solvency Declaration
